oraclePolicy.normative: Kanvas-owned generated inputs, checked-in expected dumps, stable hashes, and route diagnostics define pass or fail behavior.
oraclePolicy.nonNormative: Outside comparisons may explain drift, but they do not define fixture pass or fail results.

fixtures.generationPolicy: Use synthetic A8 masks with fixed bounds, padding, row stride, atlas size, placement order, and atlas byte hash.
Use a bounded atlas request that exceeds capacity before placement and records the stable refusal.
Use fixed cold/warm cache telemetry samples with advisory memory and upload-preparation budget overruns tied to strike-key hashes.
Use fixed A8 and SDF resident entries plus one evicted A8 entry and assert stable key preimages, hashes, resident bytes, and lifecycle tokens.
Use deterministic cold and warm cache samples with fixture timing counters, route counts, hit/miss, eviction, invalidation, resident bytes, and upload-preparation bytes.

Use a closed rectangle outline with the stabilized default spread plus an explicit widened spread, then assert inside/edge/outside facts, padding growth, normalization version, and stable CPU-only SDF hashes in the checked-in dump.
Use a perspective transform bucket and assert the stable SDF refusal plus fallback route.
Use mismatched artifact/current generation values and assert stable invalidation-token evidence.
Generate compact CFF INDEX and charstring data that calls one local and one global subroutine with bounded recursion.
Generate a charstring with a malformed operand stack and assert a stable stack diagnostic.
Generate deterministic local, global, and nested Type 2 subroutine fixtures plus refusal cases for out-of-range indexes, depth limits, missing return, and instruction budgets.
Generate one Type 2 charstring with moveto, line, curve, flex, width, and end glyph operations.
Generate a charstring containing one unsupported escaped operator and assert the precise refusal.
Generate a compact CFF2 charstring with vsindex and blend plus one deterministic variation position.

Generate a compact CFF2 table with a top-dict VariationStore offset, one VariationRegionList region, one ItemVariationData region index, and a charstring blend whose scalar differs from the raw axis coordinate.
Generate malformed CFF INDEX bytes and a top dict missing CharStrings, then assert stable table-level refusal diagnostics.

Generate compact CFF and CFF2 table bytes through the public scalers and assert stable tableEvidence dumps for format, charstring count, subroutine counts, private-dict presence, top-dict operators, and CFF2 variation-axis tags.
Generate a COLRv0 table with one base glyph and two layer glyphs plus a deterministic CPAL palette.
Use a synthetic expanded paint count over the configured limit and assert the stable `expandedPaintCount` budget refusal recorded by the COLRv1 fixture manifest.

Generate bounded COLRv1 transform/composite/clip planning fixtures that cover accepted translate, generic transform, classified scale/rotate/skew, and composite-plus-clip cases, then assert explicit singular-transform, unsupported-composite-mode, and clip-budget-overflow outline-fallback refusals without claiming dedicated transform parse-format support.
Use already-parsed PaintColrGlyph records that refer back to the starting glyph and assert the cycle path.

Generate bounded PaintGlyph-wrapped linear, radial, sweep, and variable-stop linear gradients, then assert accepted bundle cases plus explicit malformed-coordinate, gradient-stop-budget, and missing-variation outline-fallback refusals.
Use truncated synthetic COLRv1 bytes so the paint payload offset becomes unreadable and assert the parser fails closed without inventing a route claim.

Use a synthetic nested PaintColrGlyph walk beyond the configured recursion depth and assert stable outline fallback plus the `recursionDepth` budget diagnostic recorded by the COLRv1 fixture manifest.

Generate a bounded COLRv1 base glyph that walks PaintColrGlyph -> PaintGlyph -> PaintSolid and pair it with a PaintVarSolid refusal that preserves explicit outline fallback when variation support is unavailable; assert the owned bundle cases in the shared color-glyph and paint-graph fixtures.
Generate COLRv1 paint data with solid paint, gradient paint, and transform or translate paint records.
Use a glyph with no available color glyph route and assert stable unavailable-route diagnostics.
Use an emoji dispatch request with no fallback family and assert stable fallback refusal.
Use a base emoji plus one skin-tone modifier and record the expected sequence facts and dispatch route.
Use one code point with text and emoji variation selectors and record distinct sequence facts.
Use a bounded family sequence joined by ZWJ and record the expected cluster and dispatch facts.

targetSpec.section: Font sources, SFNT/OpenType parsing, and collections

fixtures.generationPolicy: Generate a face with a malformed optional table and preserve ordinary parsed face evidence plus diagnostics.
Generate a face missing or zeroing a required table and assert bounded directory diagnostics.
Use generated single-face SFNT bytes with fixed source id, content hash, table tags, and face evidence.
Use explicit scan roots only and include a generated unsupported or unreadable file case with a stable skipped-file diagnostic.
Use generated TTC bytes with two deterministic faces and assert selected face index in face evidence.
Generate CBDT/CBLC strike records with a tiny PNG payload and assert selected glyph image metadata.
Use a malformed PNG payload and assert decode-failed diagnostics with payload hash.
Use a non-PNG bitmap payload label and assert stable unsupported-format diagnostics.
Generate an sbix strike with a tiny PNG payload and assert glyph origin and payload metadata.
Use a requested glyph size with no matching bitmap strike and assert stable strike-unavailable diagnostics.
Use an SVG image/link reference and assert stable external-resource refusal with hashed reference facts.
Use one glyph-scoped SVG document containing a gradient, transform, and clip path, with bounded element summaries.
Use a small SVG document with one path, fixed viewBox, and deterministic element summary.
Use an unsupported glyph-scoped SVG feature and assert stable feature refusal.
Use a bounded use-reference loop and assert recursion budget diagnostics.
Generate fvar and avar tables with one mapped axis and assert applied normalized coordinates once avar application lands.

Generate composite glyf data with component transform, point-matching, and excessive component-count records, then assert transformed outline, component trace, metrics, path hash, and refusal diagnostic evidence.
Generate composite glyph data plus gvar deltas and assert composite variation evidence after composite gvar support lands.

Generate bounded simple/composite TrueType gvar tuples that prove single-point IUP propagation, wraparound interpolation, untouched contour isolation, horizontal phantom-point advance deltas, avar-normalized coordinates, HVAR-unimplemented warnings, and malformed tuple diagnostics.
Generate one simple glyph and one gvar tuple that moves all points at a deterministic variation position.
Generate bounded malformed loca/glyf/gvar fixtures that prove face refusal, per-glyph refusal, positive-control safe glyph dumps, and explicit non-claims around runtime .notdef substitution.
Generate one simple square glyf fixture with fixed loca, metrics, contour commands, bounds, and path hash.

Generate bounded TrueType `vhea`/`vmtx`/`VVAR` bytes that prove vertical metric dumps, absent-table fallback facts, bounded advance-height deltas, and malformed `VVAR` diagnostics without claiming vertical layout.
